programs.title: House Hunters
programs.desc: An engaged couple can't agree on their Louisiana house hunt; she dreams of living in a modern house in New Orleans that is close to her friends and the city life, but he wants a large fixer-upper that will allow him to have a faster daily commute.

programs.title: House Hunters
programs.desc: A Canadian couple want to make their move to the U.S. permanent by buying a home in Bloomington, Ind.; however, her American dream is a big, two-story property with neighbors, while he wants a ranch home with lots of land.

programs.title: House Hunters
programs.desc: Engaged college professors search for a home together in the Washington, D.C., area; she wants a spacious, single-family home in the suburbs, but he prefers a condo with little maintenance that's close to downtown.

programs.title: House Hunters
programs.desc: After 10 years of moving from country to country for work, a couple are ready to put down roots in the States; one prefers a relaxing New York suburban retreat, but the other wants to be in the heart of the action in Manhattan.

programs.title: House Hunters
programs.sub_title: Clash of Generations in South Carolina
programs.desc: A young, single entrepreneur wants to buy an older bungalow near downtown Greenville, S.C., to be close to her clients; however, her mother thinks she should buy a new, contemporary home with enough space for a future family.

programs.title: House Hunters
programs.sub_title: Big Dreams vs. Frugal Fancies in Arizona
programs.desc: A couple looks for a new home their family can grow into in Phoenix; he's willing to spend more for a super-sized, move-in ready house while she's more frugal and hopes to put a little sweat equity into a smaller place.
